---
title: "Recipes"
---

A recipe is a full bracket for one real task: inspect the relevant state, mutate
once, verify the named effect. Each recipe below is copy-paste ready and
deterministic — it does not assume the editor is in any particular state, it
inspects to find out.

Pinned to `unity-cli` **v{{< unity-version >}}**.

## Place a primitive at the origin

```{.cli name="Inspect: does it already exist?"}
echo 'var go = UnityEngine.GameObject.Find("Cube"); return go != null ? "exists" : "absent";' | unity-cli exec
```

```{.exec name="Mutate: create idempotently"}
var existing = UnityEngine.GameObject.Find("Cube");
if (existing != null) return "reused Cube";
var go = UnityEngine.GameObject.CreatePrimitive(UnityEngine.PrimitiveType.Cube);
go.name = "Cube";
go.transform.position = UnityEngine.Vector3.zero;
return "created Cube";
```

```{.cli name="Verify: it exists at the expected place"}
echo 'var go = UnityEngine.GameObject.Find("Cube"); return go != null ? go.transform.position.ToString() : "FAIL";' | unity-cli exec
```

## Confirm a subscene bakes to entities

```{.cli name="Inspect: console clean, find the SubScene"}
unity-cli console --type error
echo 'var r = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.GetActiveScene().GetRootGameObjects(); var i = 0; while (i < r.Length) { if (r[i].GetComponent<Unity.Scenes.SubScene>() != null) return r[i].name; i++; } return "no SubScene";' | unity-cli exec
```

```{.cli name="Mutate: enter play so baking runs"}
unity-cli console --clear
unity-cli editor play
```

```{.exec name="Verify: the baked entity count is non-zero" usings="Unity.Entities"}
var em = World.DefaultGameObjectInjectionWorld.EntityManager;
var q = em.CreateEntityQuery(typeof(LocalToWorld));
var n = q.CalculateEntityCount();
return n > 0 ? $"OK: {n} baked entities" : "FAIL: zero entities, baking did not run";
```

## Apply a code change safely

```{.cli name="The full edit cycle"}
unity-cli editor refresh --force --compile
echo 'return 42;' | unity-cli exec
unity-cli console --type error
unity-cli console --clear
unity-cli editor play
unity-cli console --type error
```

::: {.verify title="A recipe is done when its last line is green"}
The cube prints a position, the subscene prints a positive count, the edit cycle
prints a clean console at both ends. If the last verify is anything else, the
task is not done — re-inspect and find which primitive was used out of order.
:::

## On live execution

This page renders deterministically: the commands are shown, not run, so the
output does not depend on whether the editor happens to be open. Execution is an
explicit effect you perform in your shell, not a side effect of rendering. If
you want live execution inside the document, add a `knitr` engine and `bash`
cells — but keep the rendered codex pure, because a build that behaves
differently depending on editor state is not a build you can trust.
